articles.title: CEC Entertainment Names Chris Monroe as CFO
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese parent CEC Entertainment has appointed Chris Monroe as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer. Leadership changes at one of the largest FEC chains in the world signal potential strategic shifts in financial direction and growth planning. FEC operators and investors should watch for any updated expansion or refranchising strategies under the new CFO.

articles.title: The Rise of the Boutique Park: Smaller, Hyper-Themed Attractions Gain Momentum
articles.summary: A new wave of smaller, highly themed regional attractions is emerging as a counterpoint to massive destination resort parks, with lower capital requirements and focused guest experiences. This trend aligns closely with the FEC model, offering operators a blueprint for differentiation in a crowded market. Industry observers note that boutique concepts can drive strong repeat visitation and community loyalty.

articles.title: United Parks & Resorts Reports Q1 Revenue and Attendance Declines Amid Weather and International Slowdown
articles.summary: SeaWorld and Busch Gardens parent United Parks & Resorts saw Q1 2026 attendance drop by 171,000 guests versus the prior year, with total revenue falling to $278.3 million due to unfavorable weather and reduced international visitation. Despite the top-line softness, in-park per capita spending hit a record $40.62, up 5.3 percent, showing the value of upselling strategies. FEC operators can take note that weather risk and international travel trends are real headwinds, while in-venue spending optimization remains a strong lever.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is coming to Mt. Juliet, a fast-growing Nashville suburb, signaling continued investor confidence in the FEC sector in suburban markets. The development reflects a broader trend of FEC operators targeting high-growth residential communities outside major metros. Existing operators in the region should monitor this new competitive entry.

articles.title: Family Indoor Entertainment Centres Market Analysis Released
articles.summary: A new market analysis report on the family indoor entertainment centres sector has been published, offering data-driven insights into industry size, growth drivers, and competitive dynamics. Such reports are essential reference tools for FEC operators making investment decisions, lenders evaluating the sector, and suppliers forecasting demand. FEC stakeholders should review the findings to benchmark against industry trends.

articles.title: Supply Chain Cost Pressures Rising Due to Petroleum Resin Price Increases and Geopolitical Tensions
articles.summary: Ralph S. Alberts Co. is warning customers of significant price increases in petroleum-based resins driven by geopolitical disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which will impact product pricing across the amusement supply chain. FEC operators purchasing equipment, parts, or components with plastic content should anticipate cost increases and consider locking in current pricing where possible. This is a timely heads-up for procurement and budget planning.

articles.title: Area15 Las Vegas Names Palace Entertainment and Six Flags Veteran Bonnie Weber as President
articles.summary: Area15, the immersive entertainment district in Las Vegas, has appointed Bonnie Weber as president, bringing deep operational expertise from her roles at Palace Entertainment and Six Flags. Her background managing multi-park portfolios and driving operational excellence will be critical as Area15 continues to expand its lineup of immersive experiences. This hire signals Area15's ambition to scale and compete more aggressively in the experiential entertainment space.

articles.title: Philippines Children's Entertainment Centers Market Growing on Edutainment and VR Trends
articles.summary: A new market overview highlights strong growth in the Philippines children's entertainment center sector, driven by demand for edutainment concepts and VR experiences alongside traditional family leisure. The report points to Southeast Asia as an increasingly attractive market for FEC concepts, particularly those blending technology with educational play. Operators and suppliers with international growth ambitions should take note of this emerging market opportunity.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Market in India Showing Strong Growth Trends
articles.summary: A new report on India's indoor entertainment market highlights accelerating growth driven by urbanization, a young demographic, and rising disposable incomes. The report identifies FEC-style venues as a major beneficiary of shifting consumer spending toward experiential leisure in tier-one and tier-two Indian cities. For suppliers and concept developers eyeing international expansion, India represents a significant near-term opportunity.

articles.title: 'Skate Central' Family Entertainment Center Announced for Beckley, West Virginia
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center called Skate Central is planned for Beckley, West Virginia, bringing skating and family fun to an underserved mid-size market. New venue openings in secondary markets reflect continued grassroots investment in the FEC sector beyond major metro areas. Operators and suppliers serving smaller markets should track these emerging venues as potential customers and competitors.

articles.title: New FEC 'Gameland' Opens in Cookstown with VR, Sim Racing, and Retro Gaming
articles.summary: Gameland, a new family entertainment venue in Cookstown, Northern Ireland, has opened featuring virtual reality, simulator racing, and retro gaming attractions. The venue's mix of nostalgic and cutting-edge tech-based entertainment reflects a growing format trend in smaller FEC markets. Operators looking for attraction mix inspiration for mid-size venues will find this concept worth examining.

articles.title: Blackpool's New Tenpin Bowling Entertainment Centre Opens in June
articles.summary: A new Tenpin entertainment centre in Blackpool, UK, is now taking bookings ahead of its June opening, bringing bowling alongside broader entertainment offerings to the seaside resort market. The opening illustrates how bowling-anchored FECs continue to expand in leisure-heavy UK destinations. Suppliers and competitors in the UK market should track this new venue's programming and pricing strategies.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline Park Opens at Rivergate, Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy trampoline and family fun venue at the Rivergate retail and leisure development in Cape Town, South Africa. The opening highlights the continued global spread of trampoline park concepts into African leisure markets. Equipment suppliers and franchise operators looking at emerging market expansion should note this development.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Coral Springs Location Closes After 12-Year Run
articles.summary: The Monster Mini Golf franchise location in Coral Springs, Florida has shut down after 12 years of operation, highlighting the ongoing attrition in the indoor mini golf segment. Closures like this offer a cautionary reminder for FEC operators about lease renewal timing, evolving consumer preferences, and the need to refresh concepts over time. Industry observers will be watching whether other Monster Mini Golf franchise locations face similar pressures.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Shutters Coral Springs Location After 12 Years
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf's Coral Springs franchise has permanently closed, with operators citing the end of the venue's run after more than a decade. This is the second report confirming the closure, underscoring the significance of this story for the indoor mini golf and FEC franchise community. Franchisees and indoor entertainment operators should evaluate what lessons can be drawn about concept longevity and reinvestment cycles.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A dedicated claw machine arcade facility is going before a city planning panel for approval, reflecting the growing trend of specialty arcade concepts moving into retail and entertainment spaces. Claw and prize machine arcades have become a distinct sub-segment of the FEC space, driven by strong social media appeal and low operational complexity. FEC operators and real estate developers should note this emerging format as a potential anchor or complement to larger venues.

articles.title: Queensbury Amusement Center to Be Converted into Distillery
articles.summary: Plans have been filed to convert an existing amusement center in Queensbury, New York into a distillery, illustrating the repurposing pressures some FEC-adjacent venues face when entertainment concepts underperform. This type of conversion story is relevant for landlords and operators evaluating the long-term viability of entertainment tenants in mixed-use settings. It also highlights the competitive land-use pressures FEC operators may face in some markets.

articles.title: Wilderness Resort Announces Major Renovation of Wild WaterDome Indoor Waterpark
articles.summary: Wisconsin Dells' Wilderness Resort has announced a significant renovation of its Wild WaterDome waterpark, including a replacement of the see-through dome structure at the nation's largest indoor wave pool facility. The project, beginning August 24, signals ongoing capital reinvestment in aging indoor waterpark infrastructure. FEC operators with indoor waterpark components should monitor this project as a benchmark for renovation scope and timing strategies.

articles.title: Splish Splash Water Park Kicks Off 2026 Season with Extended Hours and Neon Nights
articles.summary: Splish Splash in Calverton, New York opens May 23 for its 2026 season, featuring more operating days, the return of Neon Nights after-dark programming, and upgraded food and beverage offerings. The seasonal programming enhancements reflect a broader industry strategy of extending per-visit value and driving evening attendance. FEC operators with outdoor or seasonal components can draw inspiration from Splish Splash's approach to maximizing seasonal revenue.

articles.title: Dollywood's Splash Country Opens 26th Season with New Neon Nights Event
articles.summary: Dollywood's Splash Country water park opened for its 26th season on May 16, introducing Neon Nights, a new after-dark event designed to extend guest dwell time and generate incremental revenue. The addition of nighttime programming at a water park is a notable operational strategy for driving attendance beyond traditional daytime hours. FEC operators with outdoor attractions can apply similar after-hours event concepts to increase revenue per operating day.

articles.title: Savannah Skaters Fight to Save Community After Roller Rink Closure
articles.summary: The closure of a Savannah, Georgia roller rink has galvanized the local skating community, who are actively campaigning to restore access to the facility. This story highlights the strong community ties that skating and FEC-style venues create, and the public backlash that can follow closures. Operators considering closures or lease non-renewals should be aware of reputational and community relations dimensions.

articles.title: New Roller Coaster 'Patriot's Run' Taking Shape at Jenkinson's Boardwalk
articles.summary: Jenkinson's Boardwalk in Point Pleasant Beach, New Jersey is installing Patriot's Run, a new roller coaster themed to America's 250th birthday, standing over 35 feet tall. The ride is replacing the Tidal Wave spinning coaster and is set to open this summer, demonstrating how boardwalk and small-park operators use ride replacements to refresh their attraction lineup. FEC operators can take note of the value of patriotic and anniversary-themed investments in driving new guest interest.

articles.title: Evel Knievel Experience Attraction Set to Open in Downtown Las Vegas
articles.summary: A new Evel Knievel-themed experience attraction is planned for downtown Las Vegas, capitalizing on the city's growing appetite for immersive brand-based entertainment concepts. The project follows a trend of IP-driven attractions launching in entertainment districts, competing directly with venues like Area15 and Meow Wolf. FEC operators and investors tracking the experiential entertainment landscape should monitor how IP-anchored attractions perform in destination markets.

articles.title: Spooky-Themed Amusement Center in Illinois Taps Niche Theming to Stand Out
articles.summary: A spooky-themed amusement and entertainment center in Illinois is gaining attention for its distinctive aesthetic that appeals to guests looking for something beyond conventional FEC formats. The venue illustrates how niche or counter-cultural theming can be a powerful differentiator in the FEC market. Operators exploring ways to reduce direct competition and build a loyal repeat audience may find this concept instructive.

articles.title: Cinema Entrepreneur Bets on Clearwater with 'World's Largest' Screen Venue
articles.summary: A cinema industry veteran is developing a venue in Clearwater, Florida anchored by what is being marketed as the world's largest screen, blending entertainment spectacle with destination appeal. Large-format screen experiences are increasingly competing with FECs for leisure time and group outing budgets. FEC operators in the Tampa Bay area and beyond should monitor this concept as a new entertainment competitor.
